name: QUO API Key Readiness

authority.localSecretCapture: G3/R3 restricted source secret handling, local only
authority.dryRunProbe: G0/R0 local readiness, no API call
authority.liveReadProbe: G3/R3 restricted read-only source readiness after explicit approval

purpose: Capture a QUO API key locally and prepare a read-only key validation path without opening webhook ingestion, CRM writes, Teams posts, or outbound phone/SMS behavior.

quoApiFactsVerified.authentication: Authorization header contains the QUO API key value.
quoApiFactsVerified.webhookNote: Webhook APIs are not enabled by this chunk; webhook signature/secret handling remains a later B10c gate.

allowed: Open a visible local window for Adam to paste the QUO API key.
Store the QUO API key only in .local/quo-ingress/quo-api-key.secret using Windows DPAPI CurrentUser encryption.
Write local metadata under .local/quo-ingress without the key value.
Run a dry-run readiness check that confirms local setup without contacting QUO.
Run a live read-only phone-number inventory probe only when explicitly invoked with -LiveReadApproved.
Write sanitized readiness evidence to inventory/m365-interaction-agent-b10.
blocked: Commit, print, chat, or document the QUO API key value.
Create, update, or delete QUO webhooks.
Send SMS, initiate calls, callbacks, replies, or any outbound QUO action.
Read call logs, SMS contents, voicemail transcripts, recordings, or real client interaction payloads.

secretStorage.revokePath: Delete .local/quo-ingress/quo-api-key.secret and revoke/regenerate the key in QUO.

sanitization: Phone numbers are redacted to last four digits.
Display names are reduced to hasDisplayName=true/false.
Raw response body is not committed.
API key is never written to evidence.

nextGate: Adam selects the exact QUO business number or internal test number.
Adam selects the first live event class.
Ingress pattern is chosen: manual bridge, no-code webhook bridge, or purpose-built adapter.
Webhook signing secret, raw payload retention, dedupe, owner, and disable path are approved.
Outbound SMS, callback, reply, and QUO API send remain blocked.
acceptance: Local key capture script exists and stores only an encrypted local secret.
Readiness probe script exists and defaults to no API call.
A live read-only probe can validate the key without writing CRM, Teams, QUO webhooks, or outbound actions.
Docs and config make clear that this is B10c.0 readiness, not full QUO ingestion.
